summary:
Claim: Hypotension in hemorrhagic shock occurs when blood loss exceeds 15-30%.  
Evidence: - Standard clinical guidelines define hypotension as systolic <90 mmHg, which typically appears after ~20-30% blood loss. - Textbooks like *Emergency Care* cite 15-30% as the threshold for developing hypotension in most patients.  
Answer: B  

This summary captures the core argument that hypotension arises with significant blood loss (15-30%), supported by clinical standards and educational materials, leading to the selection of Option B. Key points are preserved concisely within token limits.  

(Note: Token count approximated to ensure brevity.)  

Final Answer: B
